labels.Questionnaire-title: Questionnaire on production, import, export, reclamation, feedstock use and destruction of the substances listed in Annexes I, II or III of the F-gas Regulation.

intro.reporting-year-tooltip: Year to which the transactions reported in this report refer to.
intro.intro-1: The Regulation (EU) No 2024/573, requires reporting on production, import, export, reclamation, feedstock use, and destruction of the substances listed in Annexes I, II or III of the F-Gas Regulation. These reporting forms are to be completed by each:
intro.li-1: producer, importer and exporter that produced, imported or exported any quantities of hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) or more than one metric tonne or 100 tonnes of CO<sub>2</sub> equivalent or more of other F-gases. The reportable quantity of F-gases imported or exported covers bulk shipments, including F-gases shipped with equipment for the purpose of charging that equipment;
intro.li-2: company that destroyed any quantities of HFCs or more than 1 metric tonne or 100 tonnes of CO<sub>2</sub> equivalent or more of other F-gases;
intro.li-3: company that used 1 000 tonnes of CO<sub>2</sub> equivalent or more of F-gases listed in Annex I;
intro.li-4: company that placed on the EU market 10 tonnes of CO<sub>2</sub> equivalent or more of HFCs or 100 tonnes of CO<sub>2</sub> equivalent or more of other fluorinated greenhouse gases contained in imported products or equipment;
intro.li-5: company that received any quantities of quota-exempted HFCs referred to in Article 16(2);
intro.li-6: company that manufactured metered dose inhalers for the delivery of pharmaceutical ingredients containing HFCs;
intro.li-7: company that reclaimed quantities exceeding 1 metric tonne or 100 tonnes of CO<sub>2</sub> equivalent of F-gases;
intro.li-8: company that authorised the use of HFC quota to another undertaking;
intro.li-9: company that has received a quota transfer.
intro.intro-2: This electronic reporting tool is based on the format set out in Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2024/2195.
intro.confidentiality: All information provided via these reporting forms are treated as strictly confidential. No company-specific information is disclosed to the public; all company data are aggregated into summary reports before publication. The Commission and the EEA have established procedures to ensure that all of the data relating to individual companies remains strictly confidential even if it could plausibly be deduced from aggregate figures. No company-specific information is disclosed to the public; all company data are aggregated into summary reports before publication. These procedures limit the release of aggregate figures that might significantly rely on reports from less than three company groups. To this end, company groups that contribute to less than 5 % of an aggregate figure do not count toward the 'three company rule'. Company groups are defined according to affiliations to other companies as reported by both affiliates in the Company Information tab. Concerns regarding confidentiality can be addressed to the Commission or to the entity designated by the Commission.
intro.instructions: After updating and/or confirming your Company Information form (from the tab above), the "Years & Activities" tab will then be visible. Based upon your selections for activities conducted in the previous calendar year, appropriate reporting forms will be automatically available. Activities conducted in any year should be reported and submitted, no later than 31 March of the following year. For your reference, a 'Frequently Asked Questions' (FAQ) list is available from the help section of the BDR.
intro.instructions-2: Please note that for producers and importers the 1st January stocks will be automatically imported into section 4A/B/C based on the 31st December stocks data reported last year in section 4F/G/H. You have the option to manually edit those numbers.

company-info.affiliation-intro:
The European Commission and the EEA have established procedures to ensure that reported data will be kept strictly confidential. Aggregated data will not be published unless significantly relying on data reported by companies of at least three company groups. In case you wish to be treated jointly with other reporting companies as belonging to the same company group for the purpose of confidentiality analysis of aggregated data, you are invited to identify other reporting companies of your "company group".
Please note that affiliations can only be reported for companies which already registered in the DG CLIMA F-gas portal at https://fgas-licensing.ec.europa.eu/.
Please note that a reported affiliation in one company group will not be accepted unless reported here by both parties.

activities.feedstock-user-tooltip: Feedstock use of F-gases usually takes place in the chemical industry if certain F-gases are used as the feedstock to produce other chemicals. The use of F-gases as a blowing agent in foam production or as etching agent in electronics industry is NOT considered feedstock use. Please also refer to the FAQ.
activities.manufacturer-containing-fgases: EU manufacturer of products or equipment containing F-gases of Annexes I, II or III: please read information!
activities.manufacturer-containing-fgases-info-text: EU manufacturers of products or equipment containing fluorinates gases of Annexes I, II or III of the F-Gas regulation are NOT required to report in case they purchase within the EU the gases used for charging the products/equipment.

In case these gases are imported from outside the EU (or produced within the EU) by the EU manufacturer of products/equipment, the manufacturer should report as an importer/producer of bulk gases (tickboxes above).
EU manufacturers of metered dose inhalers for the delivery of pharmaceutical ingredients should report on received HFCs (see tickbox above).

sheet1.section1-heading: Section 1: TO BE FILLED IN BY PRODUCERS OF GASES
sheet1.section1-info1: Quantities shall be reported in metric tonnes with accuracy to the third decimal place (if the fourth decimal place is 5 or more, the third decimal place shall be rounded up, otherwise it shall be rounded down), separately for each gas listed in Annex I, II or III of Regulation (EU) 2024/573.
sheet1.section1-info2: Transactions 1A – 1E apply to EU producers of gases and cannot be reported for mixtures. Transactions 1F -1H apply to producers of mixtures and can only be reported for mixtures. Where an undertaking both produces gases and blends these gases into mixtures before placing on the market, it should report both on production of gases (1A-1E) and placing on the market of mixtures (1F – 1H).

sheet1.section2-heading: Section 2: TO BE FILLED IN BY IMPORTERS OF GASES
sheet1.section2-info1: Quantities shall be reported in metric tonnes with accuracy to the third decimal place (if the fourth decimal place is 5 or more, the third decimal place shall be rounded up, otherwise it shall be rounded down), separately for each gas listed in Annex I, II or III to Regulation (EU) 2024/573 or for mixtures containing at least one of those gases or for each gas or mixture contained in imported pre-blended polyols.
sheet1.section2-info2: Only bulk imports shall be reported in this section, including quantities shipped alongside equipment for the purpose of charging that equipment after import, but not quantities contained in equipment. Imports of gases contained in products or equipment shall be reported under section 11.
sheet1.section2-info3: All imports shall be reported, except goods under temporary storage.
sheet1.section2-info4: Please note that imports of HFCs in Section 2 need to be reported by country of origin in 2A, 2C, 2D, 2E and 2F. Values reported in 2B, 2G, 2H and 2I remain aggregate values. Imports of non-HFCs are reported as aggregate values.

sheet1.tr-02a-tooltip: Next to amounts released for free circulation, this includes quantities imported under special customs procedures referred to in Article 210 of Regulation (EU) No 952/2013 (external and internal transit, storage in customs warehouses and free zones, temporary admission and end-use procedures, inward and outward processing procedures).
sheet1.tr-02app-tooltip: Only imports/exports of HFCs listed in Annex I of Regulation (EU) 2024/573 should be reported in this category.

sheet1.tr-02b-desc: thereof: Amount imported into the Union by the reporting undertaking, including amounts purchased or received from other undertakings under a special customs procedure, not released for free circulation, and re-exported by the reporting undertaking contained in products or equipment (not in bulk)
sheet1.tr-02b-tooltip: For example gases imported for inward processing, charged into products or equipment and subsequently re-exported. In case the re-exports occur in the same year as the import, the respective amounts should be reported both in 2A (total imports) and 2B (re-exports within products/equipment). In case the re-exports occur in a later year, the amounts should be reported in 2A and 4H (31st December stocks) for the year of import and in 2B and 4C (1st January stocks) for the year of re-export. The type of re-exported products or equipment use shall be specified.

sheet1.section3-heading: Section 3: TO BE FILLED IN BY EXPORTERS OF GASES
sheet1.section3-info1: Quantities shall be reported in metric tonnes with accuracy to the third decimal place (if the fourth decimal place is 5 or more, the third decimal place shall be rounded up, otherwise it shall be rounded down), separately for each gas listed in Annex I, II or III to Regulation (EU) 2024/573 or for mixtures containing at least one of those gases or for each gas or mixture contained in exported pre-blended polyols.
sheet1.section3-info2: Only exports of bulk gases, including any quantities shipped alongside equipment for the purpose of charging that equipment after export, shall be reported under this section.
sheet1.section3-info3: Quantities from own production supplied to other undertakings in the Union for direct export, or own imports released for free circulation and exported either by the importer itself or supplied to other undertakings in the Union for direct export, shall be reported in reporting Section 5.
sheet1.section3-info4: Please note that exports of HFCs in Section 3 need to be reported by country of destination in 3A, 3G, 3H, 3I and 3J. Values reported in 3B and 3C remain aggregate values. Exports of non-HFCs are reported as aggregate values.

sheet1.section4-info1: Quantities shall be reported in metric tonnes with accuracy to the third decimal place (if the fourth decimal place is 5 or more, the third decimal place shall be rounded up, otherwise it shall be rounded down), separately for each gas listed in Annex I, II or III to Regulation (EU) 2024/573 or for mixtures containing at least one of those gases or for each gas or mixture contained in imported pre-blended polyols.

sheet6.section7-heading: Section 7: TO BE FILLED IN BY FEEDSTOCK USERS OF GASES
sheet6.section7-explanatory: A feedstock is any substance that undergoes chemical transformation in a process by which the chemical is entirely converted from its original composition and whose emissions are insignificant. Feedstock use of F-gases usually takes place in chemical industry if certain F-gases are used as the feedstock to produce other chemicals. The use of F-gases as a blowing agent in foam production or as etching agent in electronics industry is NOT considered feedstock use.
sheet6.section7-info1: Quantities shall be reported in metric tonnes with accuracy to the third decimal place (if the fourth decimal place is 5 or more, the third decimal place shall be rounded up, otherwise it shall be rounded down), separately for each gas listed in Annex I of Regulation (EU) 2024/573 or mixtures containing at least one of those gases.
sheet6.section7-info2: Only quantities actually used as feedstock by the reporting undertaking shall be reported here.
sheet6.section7-info3: Where hydrofluorocarbons (gases listed in Section 1 of Annex I to Regulation (EU) 2024/573 or mixture containing at least one of those gases) were produced or imported by the undertaking using them as the feedstock, the quantities used shall also be reported in section 5. Where hydrofluorocarbons were produced by the undertaking using them as the feedstock, the quantities used shall also be reported in Section 1. Where an undertaking produced or imported hydrofluorocarbons and subsequently sold them for use as feedstock to other undertakings, the quantities supplied shall not be reported in this section, but only in reporting Section 5, specifying the feedstock using undertaking, and in the case of producers those shall also be reported in reporting Section 1, specifying the Member State where the feedstock use takes place.
sheet6.section7-info4: Please note that reporting on feedstock use is obligatory only for fluorinated greenhouse gases covered in Annex I of Regulation (EU) 2024/573 (HFCs, PFCs, SF6 and Iso-C3F7CN). You may voluntarily report on feedstock use of gases covered in Annexes II and III of the Regulation (unsaturated H(C)FCs, fluorinated inhalation anaesthetics, other fluorinated substances, fluorinated ethers, ketones and alcohols, and other fluorinated compounds).

sheet6.section8-heading: Section 8: TO BE FILLED IN BY UNDERTAKINGS HAVING DESTROYED GASES
sheet6.section8-info1: For hydrofluorocarbons, the destruction technology approved under the Montreal Protocol shall be specified, differentiating concentrated sources (such as recovered refrigerants) and dilute sources (such as waste streams containing foams).
sheet6.section8-info2: The total quantities destroyed by the reporting undertakings themselves shall be reported. Undertakings which are producers shall also report on the quantities of their own production which has been destroyed, in reporting Section 1.
sheet6.section8-info3: Undertakings which are importers of hydrofluorocarbons (gases listed in Section 1 of Annex I to Regulation (EU) 2024/573, or mixture containing at least one of those gases) shall also report the quantities of their own imports which have been destroyed in reporting Section 5.
sheet6.section8-info4: Quantities sent for destruction to other undertakings in the Union shall not be reported in this section. Quantities exported for destruction outside the Union shall be reported in Section 3G.
sheet6.section8-info5: <b>Quantities shall be reported in metric tonnes with accuracy to the third decimal place (if the fourth decimal place is 5 or more, the third decimal place shall be rounded up, otherwise it shall be rounded down), separately for each fluorinated greenhouse gas listed in Annexes I, II or III to Regulation (EU) 2024/573, or mixtures containing at least one of those gases.<b>
sheet6.section8-info6: Where the exact composition of destroyed mixtures is unknown, undertakings are invited to provide their best estimate.
sheet6.section8-expl1: This Reporting Section is filled in two consecutive steps:
sheet6.section8-expl2: In the first step, please select the destruction technologies relevant to you. Please scroll down to the bottom of the page and click 'Confirm selected categories’ in order to proceed to the second step.
sheet6.section8-expl3: Based on your choice of destruction technologies, a table for reporting the respective quantities of gases will be rendered which you can fill in the second step. When necessary, you may return to the first step in order to add more or delete superfluous categories.
sheet6.section8-expl4: The identification of destruction technologies is required only from companies that destroyed hydrofluorocarbons. The selection of destruction technologies for hydrofluorocarbons given in the questionnaire below is based on the list of destruction technologies approved under the Montreal Protocol as set out in Annex II to Decision XXX/6 of the Parties to the Montreal Protocol.
sheet6.section8-expl5: For HFC-23, the destruction of virgin HFC-23 has to be differentiated from the destruction of HFC-23 recovered after previous uses (e.g. as refrigerant).
sheet6.section8-expl6: Companies that destroyed only fluorinated gases other than hydrofluorocarbons can directly proceed to the next page by clicking 'Confirm selected categories’ at the bottom of the page.

form7.header: Section 11: TO BE FILLED IN BY UNDERTAKINGS HAVING PLACED ON THE MARKET GASES CONTAINED IN PRODUCTS OR EQUIPMENT OR PARTS THEREOF PURSUANT TO ARTICLE 26(4) OF REGULATION (EU) 2024/573
form7.intro: This Reporting Section is filled in two consecutive steps:
form7.section11-notice: Please enter amounts of F-gases in the unit “tonnes of F-gas” and NOT “CO2 equivalents”
form7.intro-step1: In the first step, please select the categories of products/equipment relevant to you. Please scroll down to the bottom of the page and click 'Con-firm selected categories’ in order to proceed to the second step.
form7.intro-step1-highlight: Please scroll down to the bottom of the page and click 'Confirm selected categories’ in order to proceed to the second step.
form7.intro-step2: Based on your choice of categories, a table for reporting the respective quantities of gases and products/equipment will be rendered which you can fill in the second step. When necessary, you may return to the first step in order to add more or delete superfluous categories
form7.section11-info1: In this reporting section, importers of products or equipment or parts thereof containing a fluorinated greenhouse gas listed in Annex I, II or III to Regulation (EU) 2024/573 shall report on all gas containing imports released by customs for free circulation in the Union.
form7.section11-info2: On a voluntary basis, imports of products or equipment or parts thereof containing non-fluorinated gases may be reported, as well.
form7.section11-info3: Please keep in mind to report the gas contained in imported equipment even if you did not import F-gases in bulk.
form7.section11-info3-highlight: The gases contained in imported equipment must be selected in the „Gases“ tab.
form7.section11-info4: Quantities of fluorinated greenhouse gases listed in Annexes I, II or III to Regulation (EU) 2024/573 or mixtures containing at least one of those gases contained in the products and equipment or parts thereof shall be reported in metric tonnes with accuracy to the third decimal place (if the fourth decimal place is 5 or more, the third decimal place shall be rounded up, otherwise it shall be rounded down), by category. In addition to the total quantity of gases, the number of units shall be reported per category, unless otherwise stated.
form7.section11-info5: In addition to the total quantity of gases, the number of units shall be reported per category, unless otherwise stated.
form7.section11-info6: Producers of products or equipment or parts thereof manufactured in the Union shall not report on products and equipment or parts thereof where the contained gases were previously imported into or produced in the Union. Where a producer itself produces bulk gas in the Union for use in the Union for manufacturing their products and equipment or parts thereof, its reporting on production (reporting Section 1) shall also cover the relevant gas amounts, so those amounts shall not be reported in this Section, but only in reporting Section 1.
form7.section11-info7: Importers of products or equipment or parts thereof containing a fluorinated greenhouse gas listed in Annexes I, II and III to Regulation (EU) 2024/573 shall report on all gas containing imports released by customs for free circulation in the Union.
form7.section11-info8: Imports of pre-blended polyols shall not be reported in this Section, but only in Section 2.
form7.section11-info9: Where hydrofluorocarbons (gases listed in Section 1 of Annex I to Regulation (EU) 2024/573 or mixtures containing at least one of those gases) contained in imported refrigeration, air conditioning equipment or heat pumps or in imported metered dose inhalers for the delivery of pharmaceutical ingredients had previously been exported from the Union, and had been subject to the hydrofluorocarbon quota limitation for placing on the Union market, that shall be reported in reporting Section 12 in order to prove compliance with Article 19 of Regulation (EU) 2024/573.
form7.section11-info10: Where imported refrigeration equipment , air conditioning equipment or heat pumps or parts thereof or imported metered dose inhalers for the delivery of pharmaceutical ingredients containing hydrofluorocarbons had previously been placed on the Union market (including the charge of hydrofluorocarbons) and had subsequently been exported from the Union prior to re-import, that shall be reported in reporting Section 12a to prove compliance with Article 19 of Regulation (EU) 2024/573.
form7.section11-info11: The following categories of products or equipment or parts thereof include components intended for that specified product or equipment categories.
form7.section11-info12: Where there is a reference to rated capacity and the equipment can have different functions the rated capacity refers to the primary function of the equipment.
form7.section11-info13: Where Eco-design legislation adopted under Directive 2009/125/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council establishes a method for calculating the rated capacity of an equipment, that method shall be used.
form7.section11-info14_1: Any refrigeration, air conditioning and heat pump equipment charged with HFCs
form7.section11-info14_3: be reported in one of the following sections: 11_A, 11_B, 11_C, 11_D, 11_E or 11_F.

sheet8.section12-heading: Section 12: TO BE FILLED IN BY IMPORTERS OF REFRIGERATION EQUIPMENT, AIR CONDITIONING EQUIPMENT OR HEAT PUMPS OR METERED DOSE INHALERS CHARGED WITH HYDRO-FLUOROCARBONS, WHERE THE HYDROFLUOROCARBONS CONTAINED IN THE IMPORTED EQUIPMENT OR PRODUCTS HAD PREVIOUSLY BEEN EXPORTED FROM THE UNION AND ACQUIRED BY MANUFACTURERS OF EQUIPMENT OR PRODUCTS DIRECTLY FROM THE EXPORTING UNDERTAKING, AND HAD BEEN SUBJECT TO THE HYDROFLUOROCARBON QUOTA LIMITATION FOR PLACING ON THE UNION MARKET; AND SECTION 12A: TO BE FILLED IN BY IMPORTERS OF REFRIGERATION EQUIPMENT, AIR CONDITIONING EQUIPMENT OR HEAT PUMPS OR METERED DOSE INHALERS CHARGED WITH HYDROFLUOROCARBONS, WHERE THE IMPORTED EQUIPMENT OR PRODUCTS (INCLUDING THE CHARGE OF HYDROFLUOROCARBONS) HAD PREVIOUSLY BEEN PLACED ON THE UNION MARKET AND SUBSEQUENTLY BEEN EXPORTED FROM THE UNION PRIOR TO THE RE-IMPORT
sheet8.section12-info1: Quantities shall be reported in metric tonnes with accuracy to the third decimal place (if the fourth decimal place is 5 or more, the third decimal place shall be rounded up, otherwise it shall be rounded down), separately for each hydrofluorocarbon (gases listed in Section 1 of Annex I to Regulation (EU) 2024/573 or mixture containing at least one of those gases).
sheet8.section12-warning-header: Reporting in section 12 is only relevant for companies which used option B in a declaration of conformity
sheet8.section12-warning-body: (see Annex to COMMISSION IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2016/879). This means that you claimed not to need quota authorisation as the HFCs contained in the imported equipment had been placed on the EU market under the quota system beforehand, then exported in bulk and finally re-imported after charging into the equipment.
sheet8.section12-warning-footer: In case you did not use that option, please leave section 12 empty.
sheet8.tr-12a-desc: Amount of hydrofluorocarbons charged into the imported refrigeration equipment, air conditioning equipment or heat pumps, released by customs for free circulation in the EU, for which the hydrofluorocarbons had previously been exported from the Union and which had been subject to the hydrofluorocarbon quota limitation for placing on the Union market
sheet8.tr-12a-note: Note: 12A may apply, where the HFCs reported in 11_G were covered by HFC quota and exported from the EU in bulk before re-import in equipment.
sheet8.tr-12a-note-tooltip: The word 'bulk' does not necessarily refer to large quantities. In the reporting context 'bulk' means gas contained in gas containers, ranging from gas bottles to ISO tanks (as opposed to gas contained in products/equipment).
sheet8.tr-12a-tooltip: The HFC exporting undertaking/s and the year/s of export shall be specified. The undertaking/s having placed the hydrofluorocarbons on the Union market for the first time and the year/s of that placing on the market shall be specified.
